title: Sarah Paine - Why Russia and China can't escape geography
summary: Military historian Sarah Paine argues that global power for two millennia has been shaped by a divide between continental empires pursuing security through territorial conquest (China, Russia) and maritime powers pursuing prosperity through trade (Britain, US). The framework explains how geography constrains strategic choices and how industrialization altered this dynamic.
writeup: Military historian Sarah Paine lays out a framework dividing two millennia of great power competition between continental empires that seek security through territorial conquest (China, Russia) and maritime powers that seek prosperity through trade (Britain, US). The core claim is that geography constrains strategic choices, pushing land powers toward expansion and sea powers toward commerce. Paine also argues that industrialization altered this dynamic, reshaping how the two types of powers pursue their aims.
